paragraphs.body: In the article “The World of Scents: Why Do We Like Certain Perfumes,” MYSA reports why wearing a fragrance is so important and the powers that it holds. First, they talk about the seductiveness of different notes. This is important as when you are wearing a fragrance, you probably want to attract people. A fragrance has top notes, middle notes, and base notes. The top notes are the notes you smell when the fragrance comes right out of the atomizer and hits your skin. They usually dry down and soften pretty quickly. The middle notes of a fragrance are the ones that are the heart of the scent are usually most easily smelled after the initial dry down of the fragrance. The base notes are the notes that stay throughout the whole fragrance’s life. Secondly, they talk about how scent can be a great addition to yourself. A scent is the first thing people can potentially notice you by. If you walk by somebody and they smell you, they have already in their mind subconsciously made decisions about you. For example, you may smell seductive, intriguing, clean, and expensive. This makes you more inviting and approachable compared to somebody who doesn’t smell good. We don’t consciously think about it, but we associate the bad scents with trash, dirtiness, and un-hygienic. So when you smell bad, people are less likely to want to open up to you or talk to you. Furthermore, MYSA talks about how some fragrances are biological and cultural. Depending on your skin’s pH, a scent will smell different and last a longer or shorter amount on you than your friend. Culturally, some places like scents more than others. Many Middle Eastern countries enjoy a scent called oud, which Is known to be a strong and “stinky” scent. Lastly, they talk about what scents should be worn at certain times. In the summer, in the blazing heat, it would be better to smell citrus than something sweet and nauseating in the heat. Vice versa, you’d want to smell sweet and seductive on a date. Scent can make you feel emotions, good or bad, and affect you and the people around you. It can hold memories and emotions as well. Wearing a fragrance is not just some luxury; it is a power that can let you explore new opportunities. A single bottle of scented liquid holds so much value that many people don’t see. MYSA breaks down these aspects of a fragrance very well, and now I want to share my experiences with these aspects that MYSA highlights.

annotations.comment: nice transition! Can you make moves like this one throughout the paragraph?

paragraphs.body: For me, I have learned to value all of these aspects of fragrances over the years. I got my first bottle my freshman year of high school. Aromatic, woody, fresh, warm, spicy, fruity, herbal, citrusy, and more. I picked it because it was versatile and could be worn anywhere. Soon after, I got a new one. Spicy, vanilla, tobacco, powdery, and sweet. This was a brisk winter fragrance I wanted for that specific time of the year when it’s cold. Soon another: green, aquatic, floral, fresh, musky, and woody. This was for the summer, but these don’t usually last as long. I kept going, getting more and more bottles of cologne to add to a shelf that went from being in my closet to now a stand-alone shelf in my room that I proudly call my fragrance collection.

Fragrances have personalities just like people. Everybody can be summed up into a little 100-milimiter bottle. Some have underlying accords that you can only smell after they dry down on skin. Spicebomb Extreme by Victor & Rolf opens up from the atomizer with harsh notes of pepper and tobacco, but as it dries down has pleasant notes of vanilla and warm spice. One of my closest friends is a Spicebomb. We used to sternly compete and go at each other’s throats over who was better at baseball, but once high school came around, we became fond of each other and have been close ever since.

annotations.text: One of my closest friends is a Spicebomb.

paragraphs.body: Some bottles have the opposite effect. Drying with harsh notes, they usually don’t last long, and I’m not friends with these people anymore. I don’t normally think about these bottles. They’ve been pushed back on my shelf while others remain in the front. Some bottles are duds, and with one spray, an instant look of bitter disdain crosses the face. After studying a plethora of aromas, I started to realize what lasts and what doesn’t. For me, this bottle, specifically Versace Blue Jeans, is also an old friend from freshman year of high school who I had a brusque falling out with. The scent is known to be outdated in the fragrance community. He sits in the back left corner of my shelf, collecting dust like a bottle of fine wine aging in a cellar. I check on him occasionally but never pick him up and spray him again. Even though the scent isn’t for me, it totally works for someone else. That’s the beauty of fragrances. He has his own friends and supporters, just like other people who adore that specific scent. The bottle Is a part of my permanent collection, though, just as that friend will forever be a part of my life. Certain scents have the power to turn heads when wearing them. Valentino Born in Roma Coral Fantasy is a loud fragrance that hits with a juicy and vibrant red apple sweetness, energizing and almost triggering a smile. Valentino is my longest childhood friend. Every time he is in the room, everybody knows. This fragrance comes out on special occasions, the same way I see my friend only when we are back from college and our breaks line up or for the holidays.

annotations.text: collecting dust like a bottle of fine wine aging in a cellar.
annotations.comment: I have the same note as in your previous version :)

paragraphs.body: Sitting at the very top and front of my shelf with fingerprints all over the glistening bottles with wear and tear on the caps are those in my daily rotation. Versatile, they can be taken anywhere, seen a lot, and always dependable. This is my group of closest friends. We may not go to the same school anymore, but we still talk every day.

There are niche fragrances whose longevity, complexity, and uniqueness makes them so appealing. Fragrances like these are harder to come by because they are expensive, made in limited supply, or both. I originally had one that I saved up a long time to buy, but I have a few more now. I will use these fragrances sparingly, take them throughout the rest of my life with me, and keep them in pristine shape with their own personal stand on my shelf towering above the other fragrances front and center. These select fragrances represent my family, the most important figures in my life who will always be with me. Every person has a specific spot on my shelf, and every scent holds a special value that is unique to everybody.

Personally, the way I pick a fragrance is based off of my mood, the weather, where I am going, and who I am going to see. According to MYSA, “Perfumes have a special way of taking us through time and places, evoking emotions, and making a lasting impression on others”. This is very true. A scent can effect somebody in ways they may or may not know. A lot of the item people can unconsciously just associate a smell with you being clean or hygienic, but of course you never smell somebody and think to yourself, “oh they must be hygienic”. I recognize this power and take it into consideration every time I reach for a bottle on my shelf. A lot goes into it. For instance, if I am going to hang out with my friends just for fun, I immediately cross off anything expensive because I don’t want to wear an expensive fragrance just for it to be sweat off while playing basketball. If it is warm, I wear something more fresh and citrusy. If it is cold I will pick something with deeper and spicier notes. On the other hand, sometimes I am just in the mood for a specific smell and throw all of those standards out of the window and just wear what I am feeling at that time. A fragrance is a defining factor of your day because it describes exactly who you are and who you are trying to be at any given moment.

annotations.context_left: us through time and places, evoking emotions, and making a lasting impression on others”. This

paragraphs.body: Even though all of these bottles I’ve collected over the years are super important to me, let’s take a step back. Take away all the bottles I’ve gotten in the past years. My shelf would be almost empty. I say almost because there would be one singular bottle. It would be a mini bottle of Fred Hayman 273 Exceptional Cologne. It was given to me by my grandpop. You can’t even get those bottles anymore. My grandpop has always collected mini bottles of fragrance. They used to be free, so over the years, he collected some really nice brands like Dior, Prada, Gucci, etc. Since I can remember, I have always looked through the glass case of mini bottles he had in his room hanging on the wall by his closet door. He’d pick me up and hold me so I could reach and see all of the bottles. The Fred Hayman 273 was my favorite, so he gave it to me when I was about 5-8 I think. To this day, it is still in my collection. This is where my fascination with fragrances originated.

Since then, both of our collections have grown. I now hold over 40 bottles of fragrances because of him, and I’ve even inspired him to buy a few really nice fragrances as well. The one that comes to the top of my mind is Angels Share by Killian. A little bit ago, he asked me what fragrances I would want as a gift for the holidays and I said Angels Share. He got it for me and ordered himself a sample. He ended up falling in love with the fragrance. He bought himself a bottle soon after and now smells like Angels Share every time I see him. My bottle sits at the front of my collection and always reminds me of him every time I pick up the bottle to smell the scent. The fragrance is a sweet and boozy apple pie, with notes of cognac, spice, cinnamon, and all the other notes that make up the intoxicating apple pie smell. It is a warm and inviting scent, just like my grandpop. Because of him, I don’t consider collecting fragrances as much of a hobby as I do a passion. There is so much meaning and uniqueness behind each scent, and the power that scent holds amplifies its importance to me. In MYSA’s view, “Perfumes are a bit of magic in our everyday lives. They are transparent liquids capable of evoking memories, lifting up spirits, attracting or repulsing, boosting confidence, and leaving an impression—all with one spray to the skin.” Every time I have a bottle of fragrance in my hand, I feel the magic the bottle holds. I carry this magic with me everyday that I get to look at my fragrance collection and apply a fragrance of my choice. This is all thanks to my grandpop, who introduced me to the beauty and art of fragrance. https://www.foreo.com/mysa/the-world-of-scents-why-do-we-like-certain-perfumes#:~:text=Perfumes%20have%20a%20special%20way,a%20lasting%20impression%20 on%20others.
